questions.question: What is the primary purpose of declaring a function as virtual in C++?
options: A) To increase the size of the object
B) To improve code readability
C) To enable function overriding
D) To enhance code security
questions.explanation: Virtual functions allow for function overriding, enabling derived classes to provide their own implementation of a base class function.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true about the virtual attribute in C++?
options: A) The virtual attribute is a keyword in C++
B) The virtual attribute is inherited by derived classes
C) The virtual attribute is used for function overloading
D) The virtual attribute is used for operator overloading
questions.explanation: The virtual attribute is a special keyword in C++ that is inherited by derived classes, allowing them to override functions of the base class.

questions.question: What happens when a function is declared as pure virtual in C++?
options: A) It can be overridden by derived classes
B) It cannot be overridden by derived classes
C) It is automatically implemented by the compiler
D) It is not accessible from derived classes
questions.explanation: A pure virtual function is declared as virtual and cannot be implemented by the class itself. It must be implemented by any derived classes.

questions.question: Can a virtual function be overridden by a base class?
options: A) Yes
B) No
C) Only if the base class is derived from another class
D) Only if the base class has a virtual function
questions.explanation: No, a virtual function cannot be overridden by a base class. Pure virtual functions can be overridden only by derived classes.

questions.question: Is the order of virtual functions in a class important?
options: A) Yes
B) No
C) Only if the functions are pure virtual
D) Only if the functions are non-virtual
questions.explanation: No, the order of virtual functions in a class is not important. Derived classes can override functions in any order, as long as they implement all the base class functions.
